Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2:

This doesn't so much remove kp30.h. Really it removes some stuff from it and then renames it to linux-misc.h. It's also a huge change. Would it be better to put the module param stuff into their own change?

Patch Set 2:

the module param change is 90 percent of the patch so breaking it out would still be a pretty big patch unless we do a dozen smaller patches. I thought about creating lots of little patches but instead went for the big change since module params are rarely changed so this lowers the chance of a collision. The rest of kp30.h I used for linux-misc.h since that is only used for backwards compatibility support for older distros. I'm working to make all the files in libcfs/linux/*.h to be handers for distros that will not be present in the upstream client. Also I removed kp30.h to be in sync with the upstream client.

Patch Set 2:

A big patch that only does the module parameter stuff is much easier to review (and re-review and ...) than a big patch that does that and some other stuff.
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 3:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 3:

PPC64 Builder: build succesful

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Yes, but the commit message could be worded differently.

.. Use module_parm/MODULE_PARM_DESC instead of CFS_MODULE_PARM

Remove the CFS_MODULE_PARM wrapper and use the linux macros module_param and MODULE_PARM_DESC instead.

parents.subject: LU-7435 test: managing OST pools properly

commit.subject: LU-6245 libcfs: replace CFS_MODULE_PARAM with linux kernel module api
commit.message:
LU-6245 libcfs: replace CFS_MODULE_PARAM with linux kernel module api

Remove the CFS_MODULE_PARM wrapper and use the linux macros 
module_param and MODULE_PARM_DESC instead.

Change-Id: If551518b34195caf92435189f98b01895a0b0d24
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/19383

Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
